title: IMG registration in Australia: costs and timelines
metaDescription: Indicative costs and timelines for IMGs registering in Australia, with links to confirm every official fee. As at 15 June 2026.
summaryMarkdown: Registering to practise medicine in Australia as an IMG involves several separate bodies, each charging its own fees: the AMC, AHPRA and the Medical Board, the specialist colleges (for the specialist pathway), English-test providers, and the Department of Home Affairs for visas. There's no single fixed total. The figures here are **indicative and as at 15 June 2026** only, official fees change regularly (often each July) and your actual cost depends on your pathway, your college, the number of attempts you need, and your visa. The AMC reduced several exam fees in recent schedules, [[cite:amc-clinical-fee-reduced]] which shows how much these numbers move. **Always confirm the current fee on the official page before you pay or budget.**

sections.heading: Who charges what
sections.bodyMarkdown:
Your total is the sum of fees paid to different organisations, not one bill:

- **Australian Medical Council (AMC)**, establishing your portfolio, the CAT MCQ exam, and (on the standard pathway) the clinical exam or workplace-based assessment. See the [AMC fees schedule](https://www.amc.org.au/assessments/fees/).
- **AHPRA / Medical Board**, a registration application fee plus an annual registration fee. See the [Medical Board fees page](https://www.medicalboard.gov.au/Registration/Fees.aspx).
- **Specialist medical colleges**, on the [specialist pathway](/pathways/specialist), the relevant college charges its own (often substantial) assessment fees, which vary widely by college, so there's no single number. [[cite:college-fees-vary]]
- **English-test providers**, IELTS or OET, if you need to demonstrate English.
- **Department of Home Affairs**, visa application charges (e.g. subclass 482 or 186) where you need a visa. See [current visa pricing](https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/fees-and-charges/current-visa-pricing).

Because these are independent bodies, each can change its fees independently, confirm each separately.

sections.heading: AMC assessment fees (as at 15 June 2026)
sections.bodyMarkdown:
Indicative AMC fees **as at 15 June 2026**. The AMC reduced its exam fees in recent schedules, so don't assume an older quote applies. **Confirm every figure on the [official AMC fees page](https://www.amc.org.au/assessments/fees/) before paying.**

| AMC item | Indicative fee (AUD) |
|---|---|
| Establish initial portfolio | ~$642 [[cite:amc-portfolio-fee]] |
| Additional qualification (each) | ~$107 [[cite:amc-additional-qual-fee]] |
| CAT MCQ examination | ~$2,920 [[cite:amc-cat-mcq-fee]] |
| Clinical exam, in person | ~$3,000 [[cite:amc-clinical-fee-reduced]] |
| Clinical exam, online | ~$3,400 [[cite:amc-clinical-fee-reduced]] |
| Workplace-Based Assessment program | ~$1,070 [[cite:amc-wba-fee]] |

A card surcharge may apply, and extra fees exist for reissues and appeals. These are indicative only and subject to change.

sections.heading: AHPRA, English tests and visas
sections.bodyMarkdown:
**AHPRA / Medical Board.** Registering involves a **registration application fee** plus an **annual registration fee**. As at 15 June 2026 the annual medical practitioner fee is around **$1,058** in most states (lower in NSW), [[cite:ahpra-annual-fee]] with an application fee on top depending on the registration type. [[cite:ahpra-application-fee]] Confirm on the [Medical Board fees page](https://www.medicalboard.gov.au/Registration/Fees.aspx).

**English testing.** Expect roughly **$490 for IELTS** and **$587 for OET** in Australia as at 2026; [[cite:english-test-cost]] confirm with the provider.

**Visa charges.** If you need a work visa, the base application charge is around **$3,210** for the primary applicant on the Skills in Demand (subclass 482) visa, [[cite:visa-482-charge]] and around **$4,910** on the Employer Nomination Scheme (subclass 186) visa, [[cite:visa-186-charge]] as at 15 June 2026. Family members and employer levies add more. Confirm on the official [current visa pricing](https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/fees-and-charges/current-visa-pricing) page.

sections.heading: Indicative total cost by pathway
sections.bodyMarkdown:
**Rough planning ranges, not quotes**, excluding living costs, courier/translation, travel to exams and resit fees. Your real total will differ. **Re-confirm every component on the official page.**

| Pathway | Indicative core fees (AUD) | What's included |
|---|---|---|
| **Standard** | roughly $7,000-$10,000+ | Portfolio + CAT MCQ + clinical exam or WBA + AHPRA application and annual fee. Excludes English test, visa, resits, travel. |
| **Specialist** | highly variable (often several thousand in college fees alone) + AHPRA fees | College assessment fees vary by college, [[cite:college-fees-vary]] so no single figure applies. |
| Add-ons (any pathway) | English ~$490-$587; visa 482 ~$3,210 / 186 ~$4,910 | English test if required; visa charge if needed, plus family-member charges. |

The biggest variables are your pathway, the college's fee schedule, the number of exam attempts, and your visa situation. Build a buffer for resits and incidentals.

sections.heading: Indicative timelines
sections.bodyMarkdown:
Timelines are even less predictable than fees, they depend on exam availability, assessment queues, document turnaround, college backlogs and visa processing. Broad planning estimates only:

- **Standard pathway:** commonly **1.5 to 3+ years** from portfolio to general registration, driven by exam waits.
- **Specialist pathway:** commonly **1 to 3+ years**, depending on the college's process and any supervised practice or further exams.
- **Visa processing:** varies by subclass and circumstances; check current processing-time guidance on the Home Affairs website.

These are not guarantees. Always check current exam dates, college timeframes and visa processing times on the official sites.

sections.heading: How to budget safely
sections.bodyMarkdown:
1. **Confirm each fee at its official source on the day you plan to pay**, AMC, AHPRA/Medical Board, your college, the English provider, and Home Affairs each publish current pricing.
2. **Assume fees can change**, often at the start of a financial year. The figures here are dated as at 15 June 2026 and will drift.
3. **Budget for resits and extras**, clinical exam/WBA resits, translation and verification, travel, and family-member visa charges.
4. **Watch the exchange rate** if paying from overseas.
5. **Don't treat any figure here as a quote.** Use it to plan, then verify before committing money.

claims.text: The AMC fee to establish an initial portfolio is approximately AUD $642 as at 15 June 2026.
The AMC charges approximately AUD $107 to assess each additional qualification as at 15 June 2026.
The AMC CAT MCQ examination fee is approximately AUD $2,920 as at 15 June 2026.
The AMC clinical examination fee is approximately AUD $3,000 in person and AUD $3,400 online as at 15 June 2026, reduced from earlier schedules.
The AMC Workplace-Based Assessment program fee is approximately AUD $1,070 as at 15 June 2026.
The medical practitioner annual registration fee is approximately AUD $1,058 in most states (lower in NSW) for 2025/26, current as at 15 June 2026.
AHPRA charges a registration application fee in addition to the annual fee, with the amount depending on the registration type, as at 15 June 2026.
An IELTS test in Australia costs approximately AUD $490 and an OET test approximately AUD $587 as at 2026.
The base visa application charge for the Skills in Demand (subclass 482) visa is approximately AUD $3,210 for the primary applicant as at 15 June 2026.
The base visa application charge for the Employer Nomination Scheme (subclass 186) visa is approximately AUD $4,910 for the primary applicant as at 15 June 2026.
Specialist college SIMG assessment fees vary significantly between colleges, so no single fee applies across the specialist pathway, as at 15 June 2026.

faq.question: How much does it cost in total to register as an IMG?
faq.answerMarkdown: There's no single fixed total. On the standard pathway, core AMC and AHPRA fees often add up to roughly AUD $7,000-$10,000 or more, before English testing, visa charges, resits and travel. The specialist pathway depends heavily on the college's fees. These are indicative ranges as at 15 June 2026, confirm each component on the official AMC, Medical Board and Home Affairs pages.

faq.question: How much are the AMC exams?
faq.answerMarkdown: As at 15 June 2026 the CAT MCQ exam is approximately AUD $2,920, and the clinical exam is approximately AUD $3,000 in person or AUD $3,400 online. The AMC has reduced these in recent schedules, so always confirm the current amount on the official AMC fees page.

faq.question: What does AHPRA registration cost?
faq.answerMarkdown: AHPRA charges a registration application fee plus an annual fee. As at 15 June 2026 the annual medical practitioner fee is around AUD $1,058 in most states (lower in NSW), with a separate application fee on top depending on registration type. Confirm on the Medical Board fees page.

faq.question: Why can't you give me one exact number for the specialist pathway?
faq.answerMarkdown: Because each specialist college runs its own assessment and sets its own fees, which differ significantly between colleges. Identify your college from the colleges directory and check its current fee schedule, then add the AHPRA application and annual fees.

faq.question: How much do the English tests cost?
faq.answerMarkdown: If you need to evidence English, expect roughly AUD $490 for IELTS and AUD $587 for OET in Australia as at 2026. Prices and surcharges change, so confirm with the provider when you book.

faq.question: What about visa costs?
faq.answerMarkdown: If you need a work visa, the base application charge is around AUD $3,210 for the primary applicant on the subclass 482, and around AUD $4,910 on the subclass 186, as at 15 June 2026. Family members and employer levies add more. Confirm on the official current visa pricing page.

faq.question: How long does the whole process take?
faq.answerMarkdown: It varies widely. The standard pathway commonly takes around 1.5 to 3+ years; the specialist pathway around 1 to 3+ years, depending on college assessment timeframes. Exam availability, assessment queues and visa processing all affect this. These are broad estimates, not guarantees.

faq.question: Are these fees guaranteed to be current?
faq.answerMarkdown: No. Every figure here is indicative and dated as at 15 June 2026. Official fees change regularly, often at the start of a financial year. Treat these as planning estimates and confirm the current amount on each body's official fee page before you pay.
